paragraphs.body: In a country founded on unfairness and lies under the Declaration of Independence, in today’s time fairness and equality are now a steppingstone of the world, with groups like LGBTQ+, BLM, and even Women advocates. While there are still things that leak out unfairness and inequality there are not a lot of people who are willing to turn a blind eye anymore but there are some who would.

paragraphs.body: Recently I read an article on a sports debate in the context of James Harden Vs Dwayne Wade, Dwyane Wade vs. James Harden: The key stats you need to know in the all-time shooting guard debate by Stephen Noh. The article listed details and statistics to give a fair and unbiased decision on who is the better player. There’s a lot that goes into calling someone better whether it points, reputation, most wins, most rings, etc. But you can’t make decisions without having all the knowledge. That’s what I took from this article, if there’s anything that needs to be highlighted it is fairness when it comes to debates I always hated history I dreaded looking back on stop we can no longer change or fix, but that was always because I looked at it wrong it wasn’t about fixing the past it was about improving it in the future and learning that I started to take history for serious but for the most part social Hierarchy to be exact, throughout time there have been many examples of unjust and inequality, such as slavery, racism, even pay grade. All of these hold a big part of my brain because I take to heart anything unfair and unjust. In terms of sports James Harden is my favorite player but by no means will I ever think he’s better than someone like Lebron James just because of my feelings toward him. There’s a lot that goes into someone’s success and sometimes things get overshadowed and lead to missed details. To give an example According to Stephen Noh “The issue with statistics is that they don’t capture defensive impact where Wade has a huge edge on Harden”. In other words, Noh believes statistics and other forms of information don’t tell the whole story and if they did it could In high school I opted to sign up for this scholarship from my school, meeting all the requirements I felt pretty confident going into the interview leading to me doing well in the interviewing process, I was involved heavily in school, NHSS scholar, AP credits, Athlete, 2nd in my class, it seem like I was doing everything right and it felt like A easy scholarship to obtain, but it wasn’t until I knew who had a say so in the determining winner when my heart dropped immediately, while I was one of the best if not the best candidate for this scholarship some signed up just because of the counselor influence on them. The day came to announce the winners of the Scholarship awards and not only I didn’t get the scholarship I wanted but I didn’t hear my name called not once I do well hiding it but then people started to come out to me and started asking why didn’t they call your name, why didn’t they announce you and that’s when I knew I wasn’t crazy, and the only answer I could give them was I don’t know. I knew there was a high amount of favoritism when determining who gets the scholarships, I just didn’t know it was that bad to the point that they could overshadow someone’s credentials as esteemed as mine.

annotations.text: if there’s anything that needs to be highlighted it is fairness when it comes to debates

paragraphs.body: There are so many determining factors when debating or just trying to figure out who deserves what, but that determining factor is different for everyone. According to Stephen Noh, “Both Wade and Harden have won their fair share of individual awards”; “The date will always circle back to rings”. Basically, Noh is saying that even though there are a lot of different things you can look at to determine the debate it will always come back to one thing RINGS.

annotations.text: Basically, Noh is saying that even though there are a lot of different things you can look at to determine the debate it will always come back to one thing RINGS.

paragraphs.body: I Push the agenda of fairness and equality because people deserve at least a fair chance when it comes to opportunities and depriving some of those equal rights makes people feel less which shouldn’t be the case at all. So, every time I’m in a debate or thinking about something I make sure I give every scenario the same POV.
